reasoning: The Lean signature correctly formalizes Rudin's Exercise 2.27a. The ambient space `EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin k)` is the standard Mathlib encoding of ℝ^k; `¬ Set.Countable E` encodes "E is uncountable"; and `P = {x | ∀ U ∈ 𝓝 x, ¬ Set.Countable (U ∩ E)}` is exactly the set of condensation points of E (every neighborhood of x meets E in an uncountable set — equivalent to the usual "every open neighborhood" formulation). Mathlib's `Perfect` is defined as `IsClosed` plus `Preperfect` (every point is an accumulation point), which matches Rudin's definition of a perfect set (closed + every point is a limit point). The quantifier structure and mathematical content match precisely.
